disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden, overwhelming desire to pass urine that is impossible to defer, immediately followed by involuntary leakage of a moderate-to-large volume of urine.
trigger_question_examples: Can you tell me exactly what happens when you need to pee?
Does the leaking come with the urge or separately?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The urge hits me out of nowhere and it's so strong I can't hold it at all — then I leak a big amount before I can even get up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Urinary frequency exceeding 15 voids per day with near-hourly nocturia causing severe sleep deprivation.
trigger_question_examples: How many times are you passing urine in a day and overnight?
How is your sleep being affected?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm going more than fifteen times a day, and at night I'm waking up almost every single hour to pee. I haven't slept properly in days.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Urgency is predictably triggered by hearing running water or by unlocking the front door (latchkey/situational urgency).
trigger_question_examples: Is there anything that reliably sets the urge off?
Do you notice it worse around water or arriving home?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — just hearing running water, or putting my key in the door when I get home, and suddenly I'm desperate and leaking.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No burning or stinging when passing urine (denies dysuria).
trigger_question_examples: Is there any burning or stinging when you wee?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, there's no burning or stinging at all when I go — it doesn't hurt to pee.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No visible blood in the urine (denies macroscopic haematuria).
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any blood or pink/red colour in your urine?
Any blood when you wipe or in the toilet?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I haven't seen any blood in my urine — the colour looks normal to me.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Normal urinary stream with no hesitancy or straining (denies weak stream).
trigger_question_examples: When you do get to the toilet, is your stream normal or weak?
Any trouble starting to pass urine?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, once I get going my stream is completely normal — no dribbling or straining to start.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The leakage occurs strictly after a strong sensory urge, not synchronously with coughing, laughing or physical exertion.
trigger_question_examples: Do you leak when you cough, laugh or lift something?
Is the leak with the urge, or with physical effort?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's always with that desperate urge — not when I cough or laugh. Coughing doesn't make me leak; it's the urge that does.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: High daily caffeine intake (frequent coffee and tea).
trigger_question_examples: How much coffee, tea or cola do you drink in a day?
What are you drinking through the day?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Quite a lot of coffee and tea, to be honest — it's how I've been getting through the exhaustion.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, chills or loin/flank pain.
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fevers, shivers or pain in your back/sides?
Do you feel feverish or unwell in yourself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fevers or shivers, and no pain in my back or sides — I just feel wrecked from no sleep.
